title: Aerangis lacroixiae
taxonomy: Aerangis lacroixiae is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. This genus is part of the subfamily Epidendroideae and tribe Vandeae. Aerangis lacroixiae is classified within the subgenus Sarcochilus, which is primarily found in tropical Africa. The classification further breaks down into the family Orchidaceae, followed by the subfamily Epidendroideae, tribe Vandeae, and finally, the subgenus Sarcochilus. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the Aerangis lacroixiae species.

intro: Aerangis lacroixiae, also known as the Lacroix's Aerangis, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to Madagascar, characterized by its unique and intricate flower structure. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aerangis lacroixiae include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aerangis lacroixiae is often prepared as an infusion or tincture to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional African medicine to treat conditions such as malaria and tuberculosis.
